steps.title: Identify the contrasting findings
steps.content: The passage presents two opposite observations: actors showed suppressed brain responses during acting, but normal responses during non-acting. These contradictory findings need a transition word to show their relationship.

steps.title: Choose the transition marking opposition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second finding directly opposes the first.
  • Choice A — "conversely,": signals direct opposition between two things, so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — "specifically,": signals that the second sentence narrows or details the first — but the actors' normal responses in non-acting contexts don't narrow the acting finding, they contradict it. ✗
  • Choice C — "likewise,": signals similarity, but the two responses are opposite, not similar. ✗
  • Choice D — "thus,": signals a logical consequence, but suppressed acting responses don't cause normal non-acting responses. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
